title: WIP - unlock container before starting it via an OCI runtime
body:
If needed, unlock the container before starting it via the OCI
runtime. We do not have any control how long the starting process
may take, and there are some cases [1] where the runtime may even
deadlock. Unlocking it here, will prevent a deadlock with other
Podman processes attempting to acquire the container's lock.

In order to be able to acquire the lock in `(*Container).start()`, we
need to change the attach workflow a bit.  `attach(...)` has in some
cases beeen executed in a goroutine ultimately yielding to panics when
trying to acquire the lock since the posix semaphores used to implement
the container locks do not allow for another thread to unlock.  This
required moving the final attaching to a goroutine and passing down the
attach error channel to `attach(...)`.  A nice sideeffect is that most
complexity is now hidden from the callers.

Note that [1] is caused by a known issue when the NOTIFY_SOCKET is set.
In that case, the OCI runtime will block until the container sends
sdnotify messages.  This behaviour is legit but it's equally dangerous
for the aforementioned deadlock potential.  This change generalizes the
deadlock potential as the OCI runtimes and anything they might
be calling is outside of our immediate control and we should not
keep the lock until we return.

Further note that the Podman process will still be blocked by
`StartContainer(...)` but it won't block others anymore.

[1] https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1781506

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@rhatdan @mheon @giuseppe PTAL

@edsantiago, in case you have some downstream attach tests, we could throw them at this change. But I am fairly confident the PR is not changing any visible semantics.
Please don't restart the last test. The errors occur reproducibly there but I fail to get them locally.

Biggest potential issue I can see is that we now allow other operations
while the container is starting. Since state is still Created and the lock
is available libpod will let you unmount the container, or start a parallel
start job on the same container, or remove it.

On Thu, Dec 12, 2019, 09:01 Valentin Rothberg <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> Please don't restart the last test. The errors occur reproducibly there
> but I fail to get them locally.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4683?email_source=notifications&email_token=AB3AOCCM7REJZBOSTBMMLPDQYI7ZVA5CNFSM4JZ3NYCKYY3PNVWWK3TUL52HS4DFVREXG43VMVBW63LNMVXHJKTDN5WW2ZLOORPWSZGOEGWX7GY#issuecomment-565018523>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCEYKYMFFHUTJ5DIE2TQYI7ZVANCNFSM4JZ3NYCA>
> .
>

> Biggest potential issue I can see is that we now allow other operations while the container is starting. Since state is still Created and the lock is available libpod will let you unmount the container, or start a parallel start job on the same container, or remove it.

That could turn into some nasty situations, true. Eeeew. Seems like we're between a rock and a hard place.

I think we might want a Starting state that locks out remove, start, etc. The problem would be how to transition out of that state if we transition out of that state if the OCI runtime does block and the user Control-C's the Podman process.

> I think we might want a Starting state that locks out remove, start, etc. The problem would be how to transition out of that state if we transition out of that state if the OCI runtime does block and the user Control-C's the Podman process.

I thought about that too and am not yet sure we can solve that entirely race free. If we find some way to recover from such corruption, that'll be ideal.

I will mark this PR as work-in-progress, as there's clearly a bigger rabbit hole.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4837) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@vrothberg: PR needs rebase.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
Closing for now as there's no immediate need and ultimately not a good solution at hand atm.
